name: ghscout
vision: CLI evidence engine for product discovery from GitHub issues. Scans repos for recurring pain patterns, clusters them, scores opportunities, and outputs structured evidence packages.

tasks.title: Initialize project structure with TypeScript + Commander
tasks.description: Create package.json (name: ghscout, bin: ghscout/dist/index.js), tsconfig.json (ES2022, NodeNext), vitest.config.ts. Install deps: commander, yaml. Dev deps: typescript, vitest, tsx, @types/node. Create src/index.ts with Commander root program (name, version, description). Create .gitignore (node_modules, dist, .env, .DS_Store). Verify `npx tsx src/index.ts --help` prints help.
acceptance: package.json has name ghscout and bin field
npx tsx src/index.ts --help prints help text with ghscout name

hints: Reuse patterns from spec2cli project structure
ESM with type: module

tasks.title: Create GitHub auth resolver
tasks.description: Create src/github/auth.ts. Resolve auth token with priority: 1) GITHUB_TOKEN env var, 2) run `gh auth token` subprocess (catch if gh not installed), 3) null (unauthenticated). Export resolveToken(): Promise<string | null>. Export buildHeaders(token): Record<string, string> that adds Authorization header if token exists and always adds Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json. Write tests in src/github/auth.test.ts mocking env vars and subprocess.
acceptance: resolveToken returns GITHUB_TOKEN from env when set
resolveToken falls back to gh auth token subprocess
resolveToken returns null when neither available
buildHeaders includes Authorization Bearer when token exists

hints: Use execSync for gh auth token, wrap in try/catch
Do not fail if gh CLI is not installed

tasks.title: Create rate-limited HTTP client for GitHub API
tasks.description: Create src/github/client.ts. Export class GitHubClient with constructor(token: string | null). Methods: get<T>(url, params?): Promise<T>, getPaginated<T>(url, params?, maxPages?): Promise<T[]>. Track rate limits from X-RateLimit-Remaining header. Warn to stderr at 20% remaining. Throw RateLimitError when exhausted. Support pagination via Link header parsing. Write tests in src/github/client.test.ts with mocked fetch.
acceptance: get() makes authenticated request when token provided
Tracks rate limit from response headers
Warns to stderr when rate limit below 20%
getPaginated follows Link header for next pages

hints: Parse Link header with regex for rel=next
Use native fetch
Create src/github/types.ts for shared types

tasks.title: Create file-based cache layer
tasks.description: Create src/github/cache.ts. Cache dir: ~/.cache/ghscout/. Cache key: sha256 of URL + params. Store JSON with { data, timestamp } wrapper. Export getCached<T>(key, ttlMs): T | null and setCache(key, data): void. Repo metadata TTL: 24h. Issue data TTL: 1h. Support --no-cache flag (bypass reads). Write tests in src/github/cache.test.ts using temp directory.
acceptance: Returns null for expired cache entries
Returns data for valid cache entries
Different URLs get different cache keys

hints: Reuse cache pattern from spec2cli src/parser/cache.ts
Use createHash from node:crypto

tasks.title: Create GitHub data fetchers (issues, PRs, repos)
tasks.description: Create src/github/fetchers.ts. Export functions: fetchRepoMeta(client, owner/repo): RepoMeta (stars, pushed_at, topics, language), fetchIssues(client, owner/repo, opts: {limit, period, state}): Issue[] (title, body, labels, reactions, comments_count, created_at, html_url, user), fetchPulls(client, owner/repo, opts: {state: closed, limit}): Pull[] (title, merged, reactions, html_url, user), fetchOrgRepos(client, org, minStars): RepoMeta[], searchReposByTopic(client, topic, lang, minStars): RepoMeta[]. Define all types in src/github/types.ts. Write tests with fixture data in test/fixtures/.
acceptance: fetchIssues respects limit and period filter
fetchPulls distinguishes merged vs rejected (closed not merged)

hints: GitHub issues API: reactions come via Accept: application/vnd.github.squirrel-girl-preview+json or reactions+json
Period filter: use since param (ISO 8601)
Closed PRs: merged_at === null means rejected

tasks.title: Create text tokenizer with stopword removal and bigram extraction
tasks.description: Create src/analysis/tokenizer.ts. Export tokenize(text): string[] — lowercase, remove punctuation, remove stopwords (built-in list of ~150 English stopwords + GitHub-specific: 'issue', 'bug', 'fix', 'error', 'please', 'using', 'version'). Export extractBigrams(tokens): string[] — consecutive token pairs. Export tokenizeTitle(title): string[] — optimized for issue titles (shorter text). Write tests in src/analysis/tokenizer.test.ts with real issue title examples.
acceptance: Handles edge cases: empty strings, single words, special chars

hints: Keep stopword list inline, no external dep
Bigrams: ['auth', 'middleware'] → 'auth middleware'

tasks.title: Create issue clustering algorithm
tasks.description: Create src/analysis/cluster.ts. Export clusterIssues(issues: Issue[]): Cluster[]. Clustering strategy: 1) Tokenize each issue title into bigrams+unigrams, 2) Count token frequency across all issues, 3) Group issues that share top tokens (threshold: 2+ shared significant tokens), 4) Merge small clusters (<2 issues) into 'other', 5) Also group by shared labels as secondary signal. A Cluster has: name (top keyword/bigram), issues (list), issueCount, totalReactions, labels (common labels). Write tests with realistic fixture data (create test/fixtures/sample-issues.json with 20+ real-looking issues).
acceptance: Groups issues with similar titles into clusters
Cluster name is the most representative keyword/bigram
Each cluster tracks issue count and total reactions
Label overlap used as secondary grouping signal
Returns clusters sorted by issue count descending

hints: Start simple: frequency-based grouping. Don't over-engineer.
Cluster name = most frequent bigram in the group

tasks.title: Create opportunity scoring engine
tasks.description: Create src/analysis/scorer.ts. Export scoreCluster(cluster: Cluster, repoMeta: RepoMeta): ScoredCluster. Score 0-100 with weighted components: demand (30%): total 👍 reactions normalized, frequency (25%): issue count normalized, frustration (15%): 👎 reactions + frustration keyword count + avg issue age, marketSize (15%): repo stars, gap (15%): ratio of issues without linked merged PRs. Export type ScoredCluster extends Cluster with { score, breakdown: { demand, frequency, frustration, marketSize, gap } }. Write tests.
acceptance: High reaction clusters score higher on demand
Many-issue clusters score higher on frequency
Old unresolved issues score higher on frustration
High-star repos score higher on market size
Clusters with no merged PR solutions score higher on gap

hints: Normalize each signal to 0-100 before weighting
Use min-max normalization within the result set

tasks.title: Create rejected PR and workaround detectors
tasks.description: Create src/analysis/signals.ts. Export detectRejectedDemand(pulls: Pull[]): RejectedPR[] — closed PRs where merged_at is null AND total_reactions >= 3. Export detectWorkarounds(issues: Issue[]): WorkaroundIssue[] — issues where body or comments contain code blocks (``` markers) or npm package references (pattern: `npm install X` or package name mentions). These are extra signals fed into clustering. Write tests.
acceptance: Identifies closed-not-merged PRs with 3+ reactions as rejected demand
Identifies issues with code blocks as workaround signals
Detects npm package references in issue bodies
Returns empty arrays when no signals found

hints: Simple regex for code blocks: /```[\s\S]*?```/
npm pattern: /npm i(?:nstall)?\s+([\w@/-]+)/

tasks.title: Create output formatters for scan results
tasks.description: Create src/output/formatters.ts. Export formatScanResult(clusters: ScoredCluster[], opts: {format, top}): string. Formats: 'json' (compact JSON), 'table' (aligned columns: rank, score, name, issues, reactions, top issue URL), 'pretty' (colorized with score breakdown, emoji indicators for frustration/demand, example issue titles+URLs). Default: pretty in TTY, json in pipe. Respect NO_COLOR env. Write tests.
acceptance: json format outputs valid compact JSON
table format shows aligned columns with rank/score/name/issues/reactions
pretty format includes colorized score and breakdown
top flag limits number of clusters shown

hints: Reuse formatter patterns from spec2cli src/output/formatters.ts
Use ANSI codes directly, no chalk dep

tasks.title: Wire up the scan command for single repo
tasks.description: In src/index.ts, register the 'scan' command. Accept positional arg <repo> (owner/repo format). Wire global options: --output, --limit, --period, --min-stars, --verbose, --no-cache, --top, --min-reactions. Flow: 1) resolve auth token, 2) create GitHubClient, 3) fetchRepoMeta, 4) fetchIssues with limit+period, 5) fetchPulls (closed), 6) detect signals (rejected PRs, workarounds), 7) cluster issues, 8) score clusters, 9) format output. Handle errors: invalid repo format, repo not found, rate limit. Write integration test with mocked fetch that tests the full flow.
acceptance: ghscout scan owner/repo fetches issues and outputs scored clusters

hints: Extract scan logic into src/commands/scan.ts, register in index.ts
Use process.stderr for verbose/warnings, process.stdout for output only

tasks.title: Add --org flag to scan command
tasks.description: Extend scan command: when --org <org> is passed (no positional repo), 1) fetchOrgRepos filtered by --min-stars, 2) take top 10 repos by stars, 3) run the scan pipeline on each, 4) merge clusters across repos (cross-repo dedup by cluster name similarity), 5) output aggregated results with repo source attribution. Show progress to stderr (Scanning repo 1/10...). Write test.
acceptance: ghscout scan --org vercel scans top repos of org
Shows which repos contributed to each cluster

hints: Reuse single-repo scan pipeline
Cross-repo merge: clusters with same top bigram merge

tasks.title: Add --topic flag to scan command
tasks.description: Extend scan command: when --topic <topic> is passed (no positional repo, no --org), 1) searchReposByTopic filtered by --lang and --min-stars, 2) take top 10 results, 3) run scan pipeline on each, 4) merge clusters across repos. Write test.
acceptance: ghscout scan --topic devtools scans trending repos in topic

hints: Uses searchReposByTopic from fetchers
Same merge logic as --org

tasks.title: Create evidence command for deep-dive
tasks.description: Create src/commands/evidence.ts. Register 'evidence <repo> <query>' command. Flow: 1) search issues in repo matching query (GitHub search API: repo:owner/repo query in:title,body), 2) for each issue, fetch reactions and comment count, 3) detect workaround signals, 4) find linked PRs (search PRs mentioning issue numbers), 5) output structured evidence: total issues, total unique commenters, total reactions, sorted issue list with URLs. Support --sort (reactions|recent|comments) and --output. Write test.
acceptance: ghscout evidence owner/repo 'query' returns matching issues
Shows total issues, reactions, unique commenters
Lists individual issues with title, URL, reactions, age
Detects linked PRs for the topic

hints: GitHub search issues API: GET /search/issues?q=repo:owner/repo+query
Unique commenters: count distinct user.login across issues

tasks.title: Create trending command
tasks.description: Create src/commands/trending.ts. Register 'trending' command. Flow: 1) search GitHub for issues with most reactions, created in last 30 days, in repos with 500+ stars, 2) filter by --topic and --lang if provided, 3) cluster the results, 4) score and output. Uses GET /search/issues?q=reactions:>10+created:>DATE&sort=reactions. Support --output, --top. Write test.
acceptance: ghscout trending shows top pain clusters across GitHub
Only includes issues with significant reactions

hints: GitHub search: q=reactions:>10+created:>2026-02-21
Date calc: 30 days ago in ISO 8601

tasks.title: Add README with real output examples
tasks.description: Create README.md. Include: one-line description, install (npx ghscout), usage examples for all 3 commands with REAL output (run ghscout against a real repo and paste the output), how scoring works, how clustering works, comparison table vs Reddit tools (SaasFinder etc.), link to spec. Keep it under 150 lines. Add demo GIF placeholder.
acceptance: Shows real output examples from at least 1 repo

hints: Run ghscout scan vercel/next.js for real examples
Keep it concise like spec2cli README

tasks.title: Prepare npm publish and GitHub repo
tasks.description: Verify package.json: name ghscout, version 0.1.0, bin field, files field (dist only), engines node>=18, keywords, repository, license MIT. Add prepublishOnly script: npm run build && npm test. Add .npmignore (src, test, .github, tasks). Verify npm run build && npm test passes. Verify npx . scan --help works from project root.
acceptance: npx . scan --help shows scan command help

hints: Test with npm pack to verify published contents

tasks.title: Create program.md for autonomous product discovery loop
tasks.description: Create program.md at project root. This is a structured instruction file (like karpathy/autoresearch) that teaches an AI coding agent to use ghscout in an autonomous research loop. The program should: 1) Define a research session with configurable topic areas (devtools, ai, infra, frontend, backend), 2) For each topic: run ghscout scan --topic <topic> --output json, parse results, evaluate each cluster (is it a real product opportunity? does a solution exist?), 3) For promising clusters (score >70): run ghscout evidence to get hard numbers, 4) Write findings to discoveries/YYYY-MM-DD.md as structured markdown (opportunity name, evidence summary, issue count, repos affected, existing solutions, verdict: build/skip/watch), 5) Track a simple metric: number of 'build' verdicts per session, 6) Include a 'refinement' step: after each full pass, adjust search parameters (try different topics, broader/narrower min-stars, different periods) based on what produced the most actionable results. Also create discoveries/.gitkeep to track the output directory.
acceptance: Contains step-by-step instructions an AI agent can follow autonomously
Defines clear metric: build verdicts per session
Output goes to discoveries/ directory as dated markdown
Each discovery has: name, evidence, issue count, repos, existing solutions, verdict
Works with Claude Code: user can say 'read program.md and run a research session'

hints: Reference karpathy/autoresearch for tone and structure
The agent should be able to run this fully autonomously
Keep it under 100 lines — concise like autoresearch's program.md
The metric (build verdicts) gives the loop a convergence signal
Include example first run so agent knows expected format
